title: Avengers: Doomsday
genres: Science Fiction, Superhero, Action, Adventure, Marvel
description: The Avengers, Wakandans, Fantastic Four, Thunderbolts, and X-Men unite in the most ambitious Marvel crossover yet — facing their most formidable villain in Doctor Doom, whose iron-clad rule threatens every universe simultaneously.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Avengers: Doomsday is the biggest Marvel event film of 2026 — genZScore 8-10. The premiere and screening crowd is the most fashion-intentional superhero fan audience in the world — MCU fans dress with deep character knowledge and cultural pride. The fashion energy pulls from three directions: (1) character-inspired dressing — Black Panther's Wakandan aesthetic, Doctor Doom's dark armored villain energy, X-Men's leather and tactical looks, Fantastic Four's clean futuristic blue; (2) Marvel fan culture streetwear — graphic tees, bold co-ords, hypebeast Marvel merch styled as fashion; (3) premiere glamour — dark, dramatic, and maximally cinematic. The color palette references the film's visual world — dark forest green and gold for Doctor Doom, Wakandan purples and metallics, tactical blacks and charcoals for the ensemble. December Bengaluru cool makes layered dark fashion entirely comfortable. The villain aesthetic is uniquely strong for Doomsday — dark green, gold, and iron grey are the most catalog-relevant Doom-coded colors. Statement graphic tees, dark co-ords, tactical-inspired pieces, and maximally bold accessories define the screening crowd.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Avengers: Doomsday screening is the Marvel fashion moment of the decade — Doctor Doom's dark green and gold villain aesthetic is the single most powerful catalog direction for this film. Lead with the Doom color code — dark forest green co-ords, gold-accented black sets, and iron grey tactical-inspired pieces for both men and women. The villain aesthetic wins on premiere night — a structured black blazer with a dark green turtleneck and slim black trousers is the most Doom-coded and catalog-appropriate screening look. Women in a dark green satin co-ord with gold accessories, or an all-black set with gold chain jewellery — bold, dramatic, and cinematic. Men in a dark tactical-inspired outfit — black or charcoal jogger-suit with a structured dark green overshirt, or a clean black slim suit for the premiere glamour direction. The Wakandan influence calls for rich purple and metallic accessories — statement gold jewellery, bold earrings, and chunky chain necklaces. Graphic tees with Marvel artwork styled with dark co-ords for the fan streetwear direction. Caps and statement glasses for the casual screening crowd. The December Bengaluru cool makes dark layered dressing perfect — a structured jacket or blazer in dark green, charcoal, or black is both warm and cinematically appropriate.

app.headline: Doom Is Coming. Dress For It.
lines: Dark green, gold, iron grey — the villain aesthetic is the brief.
Every hero needs a look. Doctor Doom already has his.
December premiere energy — dark, dramatic, and fully Marvel.

social_media.headline: Doctor Doom Has Excellent Taste In Color.
lines: Dark forest green co-ord to an Avengers screening — technically the most accurate costume in the room.
Doomsday dress code — villain-coded colors, gold chains, structured black. The Avengers assemble. So does your outfit. 💚🖤

title: Spider-Man: Brand New Day
genres: Science Fiction, Action, Adventure, Superhero, Marvel
description: Four years after No Way Home, Peter Parker lives completely alone — his identity erased from everyone he loves. A full-time Spider-Man in a New York that no longer knows his name, a strange physical evolution and a powerful new threat force him to confront what it truly means to be a hero with nothing left to lose.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Spider-Man: Brand New Day is one of the most emotionally charged and culturally significant Marvel films — genZScore 8-10. The film's premise — isolation, identity erasure, starting over — resonates deeply with GenZ's emotional landscape and makes the fashion moment uniquely personal. The screening crowd is the most passionately dressed Spider-Man fan base in the world — people who grew up with Peter Parker as their emotional anchor. The fashion energy pulls from three directions: (1) Spider-Man's iconic red and blue color code — the most universally recognizable superhero palette; (2) the film's emotional tone — lonely New York streets, early morning city energy, urban casual with a melancholic edge; (3) Marvel streetwear fan culture — graphic tees, bold co-ords, hypebeast-adjacent Spider-Man merch styled as fashion. The 'Brand New Day' tagline opens a fresh start fashion angle — clean, new, and forward-looking. July in Bengaluru is peak monsoon — the screening is likely indoor, so the monsoon context affects fabric choices over silhouette. Red and blue remain the dominant palette — but the film's warmer amber and golden poster colors suggest a more nuanced approach — warm reds, rust, navy, and cream rather than primary-color cosplay.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Spider-Man: Brand New Day screening is the most emotionally personal Marvel fashion moment of 2026 — the red and blue of Peter Parker's world is the brief, but elevated beyond cosplay. Lead with a warm red and navy color-block co-ord, a graphic Spider-Man tee styled with dark denim and a clean navy overshirt, or a rust and cream combination that references the film's warm amber poster palette. Women in a red or navy co-ord with white accents, or a bold color-blocked dress in Spider-Man's palette — red top, navy bottom, white detail. Men in a clean navy hoodie with red accents and slim black trousers, or a graphic Spider-Man tee with well-fitted dark jeans and a structured navy jacket. The 'Brand New Day' fresh start angle makes clean, crisp dressing with a single bold color pop highly relevant — a white base with a single red or blue statement piece captures the film's emotional reset energy beautifully. Caps are essential for the fan crowd. July monsoon means quick-dry fabrics and avoiding white that shows rain. Web-motif jewellery and red or blue beaded accessories are uniquely relevant for the Spider-Man fan community. Socks with Spider-Man web prints — the most subtle and catalog-appropriate fan nod.

app.headline: Brand New Day. Fresh Start. New Fit.
lines: Red, navy, and the weight of a city no one remembers you saved.
Peter Parker started over. Your wardrobe can too.
The most emotionally devastating Marvel film deserves your most intentional outfit.

social_media.headline: New York Forgot Him. His Fit Remembers.
lines: Spider-Man erased himself from everyone's memory but wore red and blue anyway. Respect the commitment.
Brand New Day dress code — red and navy, clean silhouette, one bold color pop. A fresh start never looked this good. 🕷️❤️

title: Dune: Part Three
description: The epic conclusion arrives — Emperor Paul Atreides confronts the devastating consequences of his rise to power as political conspiracies and a galaxy-spanning holy war threaten the very future he alone can foresee. The desert has its reckoning.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Dune: Part Three is the cinematic event of December 2026 — genZScore 7-9. Dune has one of the most visually distinctive and fashion-forward film aesthetics in modern cinema — Denis Villeneuve's world is a masterclass in desert minimalism meets imperial grandeur. The screening crowd is culturally sophisticated, aesthetically aware, and deeply invested in the film's visual language. The fashion energy is uniquely specific to Dune's world — desert sand tones, deep olive and earth browns, stark blacks and whites, architectural silhouettes, and the flowing draped quality of Fremen stillsuits and Atreides formal wear. The film's color palette from the poster — warm desert amber, deep forest green — confirms the dominant fashion directions. Three distinct styling modes: (1) Fremen desert aesthetic — sand, rust, terracotta, olive, flowing silhouettes, layered textures; (2) Imperial Atreides — clean architectural black and white, structured tailoring, quiet power dressing; (3) the Dune fan intellectual — the most consciously dressed literary sci-fi fan community. December Bengaluru cool makes dramatic layered dressing perfect. The Dune aesthetic is the most naturally fashion-forward sci-fi world — it requires no cosplay, just the right color palette and silhouette philosophy.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Dune: Part Three is the most aesthetically intentional screening moment of December — the desert palette IS the fashion brief. The Dune world has two distinct dressing registers and both are catalog-relevant. Fremen desert aesthetic — women in flowing wide-leg linen trousers in sand or rust with a cream or terracotta draped top, or a flowing midi dress in desert tones with interesting layered textures. Men in a relaxed olive or sand linen shirt with wide-leg cream or brown trousers — the Fremen scholar meets Bengaluru intellectual. Imperial Atreides aesthetic — a clean all-black tailored suit or structured black blazer with black trousers for the imperial power dressing direction. The contrast between sand and black — Fremen versus Harkonnen, desert versus space — is the most powerful Dune fashion binary and both are equally valid screening looks. The December Bengaluru cool makes heavy linen and cotton-linen blend layering genuinely comfortable — a linen overshirt in deep olive over a cream turtleneck is peak Dune dressing. Minimal artisanal jewellery in gold and bronze tones — simple rings, a thin chain, small geometric earrings. Statement glasses for the literary sci-fi intellectual crowd. Canvas tote bags — the Dune fan always carries a book. The overall energy is — dressed like you understand that the spice must flow, and so must your outfit.

app.headline: The Spice Flows. So Does This Fit.
lines: Sand, rust, olive, black — the Dune palette is the brief.
Fremen desert layers or imperial Atreides black — both are correct.
The epic conclusion deserves the most considered outfit of December.

social_media.headline: Emperor Paul Would Approve Of This Fit.
lines: Dune: Part Three dress code — desert sand tones or all-black imperial power dressing. There is no middle ground on Arrakis.
The spice extends life. The right linen co-ord extends the evening. Dune Part Three is finally here. 🏜️✨

title: Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu
genres: Science Fiction, Action, Adventure, Space Opera
description: Din Djarin and Grogu return to the big screen — as the fledgling New Republic battles the remnants of a scattered Empire, the galaxy's most beloved bounty hunter and his young apprentice navigate a universe where the old ways and new beginnings collide in the most heartfelt Star Wars story ever told.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: The Mandalorian and Grogu is one of the most culturally beloved Star Wars properties — genZScore 7-9. The Mandalorian fan base is emotionally invested, aesthetically aware, and spans generations — from original Star Wars fans to new GenZ audiences. The fashion energy is uniquely specific to Din Djarin's world — dark tactical, space western, Mandalorian beskar armor aesthetics, and the warm emotional undertone of a found-family story. The screening crowd dresses with deep affection — Mandalorian-coded fashion is the brief. The color palette from the poster — cool grey, deep charcoal, and dark near-black — perfectly captures the Mandalorian's dark metallic armor language. Three distinct styling directions: (1) Mandalorian armor-inspired — dark grey, charcoal, slate, and metallic silver with tactical details; (2) space western — dark earth tones, cargo details, and the frontier aesthetic of the outer rim; (3) Grogu soft energy — the most beloved character in Star Wars history opens a soft, warm, adorable fashion counter-direction. May in Bengaluru is intense heat — lightweight breathable fabrics are essential for the screening crowd. The dark metallic palette works beautifully even in summer when executed in lightweight polyester and cotton.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: The Mandalorian and Grogu screening is the most emotionally warm tactical dressing moment of May — This Is The Way applies to outfit choices too. Lead with the Mandalorian's dark beskar palette — charcoal grey, slate, and silver metallic in lightweight summer-appropriate fabrics. A charcoal grey co-ord in polyester or microfiber, a dark tactical-inspired outfit with cargo details, or a clean all-black set with silver accessories captures the Mandalorian armor aesthetic without the actual armor. Women in a dark grey or charcoal co-ord with silver jewellery, or a slate-toned dress with metallic accessories — cool, considered, and perfectly Mandalorian-coded. Men in a dark charcoal or slate grey tactical-inspired outfit — cargo trousers with a clean fitted dark tee and a structured dark jacket. The space western direction opens earth-toned options — brown leather-look jacket over a cream tee with dark cargo trousers references the outer rim frontier aesthetic. May Bengaluru heat means lightweight polyester and microfiber for the dark palette — both breathable and fast-drying for the monsoon transition. The Grogu counter-direction is equally valid and catalog-relevant — soft green and warm cream pieces with character-adjacent cute accessories for the fans who dress for the heart of the story rather than the armor. Caps in dark tones. Chain jewellery in silver. The overall energy is — This Is The Way, and the way is a very well-considered dark outfit.

app.headline: This Is The Way To Dress Tonight.
lines: Charcoal, slate, and silver — the Mandalorian palette needs no explanation.
Dark tactical or soft Grogu energy — both are valid screening choices.
The galaxy's best bounty hunter has excellent taste in dark greys.

social_media.headline: Grogu Approves. The Outfit Does Not Miss.
lines: Mandalorian screening dress code — dark charcoal, cargo details, silver accessories. This is the way.
Din Djarin never takes off his helmet but you can take off in this charcoal co-ord. The Mandalorian and Grogu hits screens May 22nd. 🪖✨

title: The Odyssey
description: The greatest journey ever told returns to the screen — Odysseus, legendary King of Ithaca, battles monsters, gods, and his own longing across a decade of perilous sea voyages to return home after the Trojan War, facing the Cyclops, the Sirens, and the witch-goddess Circe in Christopher Nolan's most ambitious epic.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: The Odyssey is Christopher Nolan's most ambitious epic — genZScore 6-8. The screening crowd is the most culturally sophisticated and aesthetically intentional of any summer blockbuster — Nolan fans dress with intellectual intention, and the Greek mythology angle adds a rich fashion reference layer. The poster colors — deep ocean blue and near-midnight black — immediately establish the film's visual language. Character fashion references are strong: (1) Odysseus — the warrior-king aesthetic, battle-worn leather, deep navy and ocean blue, tactical but regal; (2) Penelope — elegant draped silhouettes, ivory and cream, classical Greek draping references in contemporary cuts; (3) Circe — the witch-goddess angle, deep forest green, mystical and powerful; (4) The Sirens — ethereal, sheer, ocean-toned, otherworldly. July in Bengaluru is peak monsoon — indoor screening, breathable fabrics essential. The ocean palette dominates — deep blue, midnight navy, teal, sea green, and storm grey. The mythology angle opens a unique fashion direction — draped silhouettes, toga-inspired cuts, classical references in contemporary fashion. This is not cosplay — it is the Odyssey's world translated into catalog-matchable pieces.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: The Odyssey screening is the most mythologically inspired fashion moment of July — the ocean is the palette and ancient Greece is the silhouette reference. Lead with the deep ocean blue and midnight navy that dominates the film's visual world — a deep navy satin midi dress, a teal draped co-ord, or a midnight blue wide-leg suit captures the Odyssey's sea-voyage energy beautifully. The Penelope character direction — ivory and cream draped silhouettes — is the most catalog-translatable mythology reference. Women in a cream or ivory flowy midi dress with a one-shoulder or cowl neckline, referencing classical Greek draping in a completely contemporary silhouette. A deep ocean blue satin dress or a teal asymmetric hemline dress channels the film's visual palette directly. The Circe character direction — deep forest green with mystical accessories — is the most dramatic and editorial option for the evening crowd. Men in a deep navy linen suit or a midnight blue shirt with cream linen trousers — the scholar-warrior aesthetic of Odysseus translated into a Bengaluru screening look. The one-shoulder and off-shoulder necklines are uniquely relevant — the most mythology-adjacent contemporary neckline in the catalog. Gold and bronze-toned jewellery references the ancient world beautifully — layered gold chains, statement coin-inspired pendants, chunky gold cuffs. The July monsoon means breathable fabrics over heavy ones — satin and chiffon-feel in ocean tones are both cool and cinematic.

app.headline: Defy The Gods. Dress For The Ocean.
lines: Midnight navy, ocean teal, ivory drape — the Odyssey palette is everything.
Penelope's draped elegance or Circe's forest green power — both are correct.
The greatest journey ever told deserves the most intentional outfit of July.

social_media.headline: Ten Years To Get Home. Worth The Outfit.
lines: The Odyssey dress code — deep ocean blue, ivory draped silhouettes, gold jewellery. Odysseus traveled ten years for this screening and so did your outfit.
Circe would turn you into something magnificent in that teal dress. The Odyssey arrives July 17th. 🌊⚡

title: Masters of the Universe
genres: Action, Fantasy, Superhero, Science Fiction, Sword and Sorcery
description: Prince Adam returns to a shattered Eternia after 15 years — the Sword of Power calls him home to face Skeletor's fiendish rule, reunite with Teela and Man-At-Arms, and embrace his ultimate destiny as He-Man, the most powerful man in the universe.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Masters of the Universe is a major fantasy action film with a deeply nostalgic and maximalist visual identity — genZScore 7-9. The He-Man universe is one of the most visually bold and color-saturated fantasy worlds — the screening crowd spans nostalgic millennials who grew up with the original cartoon and GenZ fans discovering it fresh. The character aesthetics are uniquely catalog-rich: (1) He-Man/Prince Adam — the most physically powerful character in the universe, his aesthetic is bold primary colors — sky blue, gold, and the iconic cross harness. Translated to fashion: structured pieces in royal blue, gold accents, powerful silhouettes; (2) Skeletor — the most camp and fashion-forward villain in fantasy history, his purple and black aesthetic with bone motifs is genuinely editorial; (3) Teela — warrior-woman energy, red and gold, powerful and athletic; (4) She-Ra adjacent — pink, gold, and ethereal feminine power. The overall color palette is maximally bold — royal blue, deep purple, gold, red, black, and silver. June in Bengaluru is peak monsoon — indoor screening, breathable fabrics essential. The sword-and-sorcery genre opens fantasy-inspired fashion references — statement accessories, bold color-blocking, and the kind of maximalist dressing that matches Eternia's visual scale.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Masters of the Universe screening is the most maximally bold and color-saturated fashion moment of June — Eternia's visual language gives direct catalog direction. Four character-inspired directions all work beautifully: He-Man direction — royal blue and gold color-blocking is the most powerful catalog call. A royal blue co-ord with gold accessories, or a structured navy suit with bold gold chain jewellery channels Prince Adam's destiny energy. Women in a royal blue satin co-ord or a bold color-blocked blue and gold dress — by the power of Greyskull, this is the fit. Skeletor direction — the most fashion-forward option for those who root for the villain. Deep purple and black with silver accessories — a purple co-ord or a black set with purple accents is genuinely editorial and very Skeletor-coded. A structured black blazer with deep purple trousers and silver chain jewellery is the most sophisticated Masters of the Universe screening look. Teela direction — red and gold warrior energy, athletic but powerful. She-Ra direction — pink, gold, and ethereal for those who want the princess of power aesthetic. Bold statement jewellery is non-negotiable — chunky chains, large hoops, stacked rings in gold or silver. Caps in bold colors. The June monsoon means breathable fabrics in bold colors — polyester and cotton blends hold saturated colors beautifully in humidity.

app.headline: By The Power Of Greyskull, Dress Up.
lines: Royal blue and gold or deep purple and black — He-Man vs Skeletor, your call.
Eternia's most powerful characters have the most catalog-matchable color palettes.
The most powerful man in the universe deserves the most powerful outfit in the room.

social_media.headline: He-Man Blue Or Skeletor Purple. Pick A Side.
lines: Masters of the Universe screening dress code — royal blue and gold if you're a hero, deep purple and black if you're not. Both look incredible.
I Have The Power — and apparently also this royal blue co-ord with gold chains. Masters of the Universe, June 5th. ⚔️👑

title: Toy Story 5
description: Woody, Buzz, Jessie and the whole gang are back — but playtime has a new enemy. When Bonnie becomes obsessed with her new Lilypad tablet, the toys must go head to head with the biggest threat to childhood imagination they've ever faced in Pixar's most timely and heartfelt adventure yet.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Toy Story 5 is Pixar's most culturally anticipated family film of 2026 — genZScore 7-9. The screening crowd is uniquely broad — families with young children, nostalgic millennials and GenZ who grew up with Toy Story, and the broader Pixar fan community. The fashion energy is joyful, nostalgic, and deeply playful — this is the one film where the most fun and character-referential dressing is not just acceptable but celebrated. Character fashion references are exceptionally strong: (1) Woody — cowboy aesthetic, brown and yellow plaid, denim, cowboy hat, sheriff star; (2) Buzz Lightyear — the most iconic color combination in animation — white, purple, and green with space ranger details; (3) Jessie — red hair energy, white shirt with red accents, yellow hat, cowgirl fringe; (4) Rex — dinosaur green, the most fun character print direction. The Toy Story palette is maximally bright and primary — yellow, red, blue, green, purple, and white. June in Bengaluru is peak monsoon — indoor screening, breathable lightweight fabrics. The nostalgia angle is uniquely powerful — this crowd dresses for the childhood memory as much as the film itself. Kids categories are critically relevant — this is a family film and little ones dressed in character-inspired outfits are the most heartwarming catalog moment.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Toy Story 5 screening is the most joyfully dressed film event of June — four iconic character directions all translate beautifully into catalog pieces. Woody direction — the most wearable character aesthetic. A yellow and brown plaid or gingham shirt with denim jeans and a cowboy cap — the sheriff of our hearts never looked more catalog-appropriate. The brown and yellow color combination is uniquely Woody-coded. Buzz Lightyear direction — the most bold and graphic option. White and purple color-blocking with green accents — a white co-ord with purple accessories, or a purple tee with white straight-fit jeans. Star detail accessories are the most Buzz-appropriate jewellery call. Jessie direction — women in a white top with red accents and a yellow cap — the cowgirl energy is strong and very on-brand. Rex direction — the most fun option — dinosaur green graphic tees or a bright green co-ord for the fans who love the most anxious toy in the box. Kids categories are critically relevant — onesies and character-print tees for little ones are the most heartwarming catalog moment of the film. The June monsoon means breathable cotton and lightweight polyester — the bright primary palette holds beautifully in these fabrics. Character print socks are a strong subtle nod. Mobile accessories for the tech-meets-toys narrative of the film. The overall energy is — dressed like you never stopped believing that toys come alive when you leave the room.

app.headline: To Infinity And Beyond Your Wardrobe.
lines: Woody plaid, Buzz purple, Jessie red — four iconic looks, one film.
The nostalgia hits different when your outfit matches the memory.
Pixar's most joyful film deserves the most colorful screening fit.

social_media.headline: You've Got A Friend In This Outfit.
lines: Toy Story 5 dress code — Woody's plaid shirt and denim, Buzz's white and purple, or just wear the most colorful thing you own and let the toys decide.
There's a snake in my boot and a Toy Story 5 screening on my calendar. The yellow and brown plaid shirt comes out of retirement. ⭐🤠

title: Supergirl
genres: Science Fiction, Superhero, Action, Adventure, DC
description: Kara Zor-El is done waiting to be saved — when a ruthless adversary strikes too close to home, Supergirl embarks on an epic interstellar journey of vengeance and justice, proving that the last daughter of Krypton is the most powerful force in any universe she chooses to enter.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Supergirl: Woman of Tomorrow is one of DC's most anticipated female-led superhero films — genZScore 8-10. The tagline 'Truth. Justice. Whatever.' perfectly captures the energy of the film and its audience — unapologetically powerful, slightly irreverent, and deeply GenZ. The poster colors — a striking rust-red and electric blue — directly reference Kara's iconic costume palette and give immediate catalog direction. The screening crowd is overwhelmingly GenZ women and gender-fluid fans who dress with maximum intention. Character fashion references are uniquely strong: (1) Supergirl/Kara Zor-El — the iconic red and blue superhero palette, but filtered through her 'Woman of Tomorrow' grittier aesthetic — this is not the polished Superman blue but a more raw, battle-worn, emotionally charged version of the same colors; (2) the interstellar journey angle — cosmic, bold, and otherworldly accessories; (3) the female power dressing direction — confident, structured, and unapologetic. The rust-red and royal blue combination from the poster is the most direct catalog color signal. June in Bengaluru is peak monsoon — indoor screening, lightweight breathable fabrics. Pride Month context (June 26th) adds an inclusive fashion celebration layer — Supergirl's female-led empowerment energy resonates deeply with Pride Month's values.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Supergirl screening is the most powerfully dressed female superhero fashion moment of June — the rust-red and royal blue palette gives direct and bold catalog direction. Three character-inspired fashion directions all work beautifully. Kara's costume direction — the most direct catalog call. A royal blue and red color-block co-ord, a cobalt blue dress with red accessories, or a rust-red top with navy wide-leg trousers captures the Supergirl palette without the cape. The color-block co-ord in blue and red is the single most catalog-relevant Supergirl piece. Power dressing direction — a structured cobalt blue blazer with slim black trousers and silver accessories references the 'Woman of Tomorrow' confident energy — dressed like she owns every room in every galaxy. Women in a bold royal blue fitted dress or a rust-red satin co-ord with silver chain jewellery — the most editorial Supergirl screening look. Men and gender-fluid dressers in a blue and red color-blocked co-ord or a bold graphic tee with strong silhouette. Star and cosmic jewellery are uniquely relevant — star pendants, geometric silver pieces, and bold statement earrings. The Pride Month timing makes this screening uniquely celebratory — rainbow and bold multi-color accessories alongside the Supergirl palette. Nail art is critically relevant for the female-led fan base. June monsoon means lightweight fabrics in bold colors — polyester and spandex blends hold the Supergirl palette beautifully.

app.headline: Truth. Justice. This Outfit.
lines: Royal blue and rust red — Kara's palette, your most powerful look.
Woman of Tomorrow dresses like she already owns today.
The last daughter of Krypton has excellent color coordination.

social_media.headline: Supergirl Said Whatever. Her Outfit Said Everything.
lines: Truth. Justice. Whatever. — but specifically this cobalt blue co-ord with silver chains.
Kara Zor-El flew across the galaxy in red and blue. The least you can do is wear it to the screening. Supergirl June 26th. 🦸‍♀️💙❤️

title: Mortal Kombat II
genres: Action, Fantasy, Adventure, Martial Arts, Fighting
description: The champions of Earthrealm return — now joined by the legendary Johnny Cage — in the ultimate no-holds-barred battle against Shao Kahn's dark rule. Flawless victory or total annihilation: the fate of every realm hangs on the outcome of the most brutal tournament ever fought.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Mortal Kombat II is one of the most visually distinctive and character-rich action franchises — genZScore 8-9. The MK fan base is passionate, gaming-culture-native, and dresses with deep character knowledge. The screening crowd spans hardcore gamers, action film enthusiasts, and GenZ streetwear fans who treat the MK aesthetic as a fashion reference. Character fashion references are exceptionally strong and catalog-translatable: (1) Scorpion — the most iconic MK character, yellow and black ninja aesthetic, fire energy, chains; (2) Sub-Zero — ice-cold blue and black, the most visually striking character, deep navy and steel blue; (3) Johnny Cage — the most fashion-forward MK character by far, Hollywood action star energy, sunglasses, tight black tee, signature green shorts, very GenZ streetwear adjacent; (4) Shao Kahn — dark imperial villain, deep maroon, black, and gold; (5) Kitana — royal blue with fan weapons, elegant but deadly. The poster colors — dusty rose-grey and near-black — suggest a more muted editorial approach to the otherwise maximally bold MK palette. May in Bengaluru is intense heat — lightweight breathable fabrics for the screening crowd. The martial arts angle opens a strong athleisure and tactical fashion direction alongside the character-coded streetwear.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Mortal Kombat II screening is the most character-coded gaming fashion moment of May — five iconic fighters give direct catalog direction and the crowd knows exactly which character they're representing. Scorpion direction — the most popular and most catalog-translatable. Yellow and black color-blocking is the brief — a yellow graphic tee with black cargo trousers, or a yellow and black co-ord with chain jewellery. The chain necklace is the single most Scorpion-coded accessory in the catalog. Sub-Zero direction — the most editorial and visually striking option. Deep navy and steel blue with black — a navy co-ord with icy silver accessories, or an all-navy set with metallic chain jewellery. Sub-Zero's ice palette in May's heat is a genuinely refreshing aesthetic choice. Johnny Cage direction — the most GenZ streetwear-adjacent character in MK history. Cage's Hollywood action star energy translates to a fitted black tee, signature green shorts or trousers, and bold gold-framed sunglasses — the most fashion-forward screening look. Shao Kahn direction — dark imperial villain energy. Deep maroon and black with gold accents — a structured maroon blazer with black trousers and gold chain. Kitana direction — royal blue elegant power dressing for women. Bold chain jewellery is the universal MK accessory — every character has chains in some form. Dragon motif pieces are uniquely relevant for the franchise. May heat means lightweight fabrics — polyester and cotton blends in bold colors stay vibrant and breathable.

app.headline: Prepare For The Next Level Of Dressing.
lines: Scorpion yellow, Sub-Zero blue, or Johnny Cage green — pick your fighter, pick your fit.
The most character-coded screening of May needs the most intentional outfit.
Flawless victory goes to whoever wears the chain necklace tonight.

social_media.headline: Finish Him. With This Outfit.
lines: Mortal Kombat II dress code — Scorpion's yellow and black, Sub-Zero's navy and silver, or Johnny Cage's sunglasses-and-attitude. Choose your fighter.
Get over here — and bring the yellow and black co-ord. Mortal Kombat II hits screens May 8th. 🔥🧊

title: The Hunger Games: Sunrise on the Reaping
genres: Science Fiction, Action, Adventure, Post-Apocalyptic, Dystopian
description: Return to Panem 24 years before Katniss Everdeen — the 50th Hunger Games, the Second Quarter Quell, where the rules change and the stakes double. A new generation of tributes faces a Capitol more brutal and creative in its cruelty than ever before.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: The Hunger Games: Sunrise on the Reaping is one of the most culturally significant YA franchise films of 2026 — genZScore 8-9. The Hunger Games has one of the most fashion-forward and politically charged aesthetics in cinema — the Capitol's maximalist excess versus the Districts' austere survival dressing creates a uniquely rich fashion binary. The screening crowd is overwhelmingly GenZ — deeply invested in the franchise's themes of rebellion, identity, and resistance. The poster colors — pale sage grey-green and dark warm brown — perfectly capture the film's pre-dawn reaping aesthetic. Character and world fashion references: (1) District tributes — the most emotionally resonant direction, dark earth tones, worn denim, practical layers, survival aesthetic; (2) Capitol fashion — the most maximally experimental direction, bold colors, avant-garde silhouettes, theatrical accessories — for fans who dress for the villain's world; (3) Haymitch Abernathy (the central character of this prequel) — a younger, rougher District 12 aesthetic, worn and working-class but with an underlying defiance. November in Bengaluru is the most pleasant weather — perfect for the dark layered dystopian dressing this film inspires. The rebellion angle resonates strongly with the GenZ political consciousness — dressing as resistance is a deeply on-brand fashion moment.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Hunger Games: Sunrise on the Reaping screening is the most politically charged and dystopian-coded fashion moment of November — two completely distinct dressing directions both work brilliantly. District survival aesthetic — the most emotionally resonant and catalog-translatable direction. Dark earth tones, worn denim, and practical layering in charcoal, olive, and deep brown capture District 12's aesthetic without looking like a costume. A worn dark denim jacket over a plain dark tee with cargo trousers — the Haymitch Abernathy tribute look for the modern Bengaluru screening crowd. Women in an oversized dark olive jacket with straight-fit distressed dark jeans or a dark earth-tone co-ord with minimal jewellery — the district girl who won't be broken. Arrow motif and mockingjay-adjacent jewellery — simple arrow pendants, minimal chain necklaces, plain rings — are the most franchise-coded accessories. Capitol maximalism direction — for fans who dress for the excess rather than the resistance. Bold, theatrical, and avant-garde — a striking color-blocked blazer in unexpected combinations, theatrical accessories, and the kind of dressing that says 'I belong in the Capitol and I know it.' November Bengaluru cool makes the full dark layered dystopian look genuinely comfortable — heavy denim, canvas jackets, and wool-blend pieces all work beautifully. Distressed and worn denim is the most catalog-relevant fabric treatment for this film. The rebellion energy is the through-line — dressed like the odds are never in your favor but your outfit definitely is.

app.headline: The Reaping Is Here. Dress For It.
lines: District earth tones or Capitol maximalism — Panem has two dress codes.
Dark olive, worn denim, arrow jewels — the Hunger Games aesthetic is back.
November cool was made for the most dystopian screening of the year.

social_media.headline: These Games Are Going To Be Different. So Is The Outfit.
lines: Hunger Games screening dress code — District survival layers or Capitol excess. There is no in-between in Panem and there shouldn't be in your wardrobe either.
May the odds be ever in your favor — and may your dark olive cargo jacket be the most intentional thing in the screening room. Sunrise on the Reaping, November 20th. 🏹🔥

title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
description: Mario and Luigi blast off into the stars again — Bowser Jr. is determined to free his father and restore the Koopa family legacy, sending the brothers on their most cosmic and colorful adventure yet across galaxies old and new.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is the most joyfully colorful family film of April 2026 — genZScore 7-9. The Mario franchise has one of the most universally recognized and fashion-translatable character palettes in all of pop culture. The screening crowd spans families with young children, gaming-native GenZ, and nostalgic millennials who grew up with Galaxy on the Wii. The poster colors — dusty purple-lavender and deep midnight navy-purple — directly reference the cosmic galaxy aesthetic of the film's setting, giving a more sophisticated fashion direction beyond just the primary red and blue of Mario's classic look. Character fashion references are exceptionally rich: (1) Mario — the iconic red and blue, red cap with M, classic dungarees; (2) Luigi — green and blue, quieter but equally strong; (3) Princess Peach — pink, gold, and regal; (4) Bowser Jr. — the new antagonist, orange-yellow, spiky energy, and a kid villain's maximalist chaos aesthetic; (5) Rosalina — the Galaxy's signature character, powder blue and silver, ethereal and cosmic. The galaxy setting opens a cosmic color direction — deep purples, midnight blue, silver, and star-inspired accessories. April in Bengaluru is warm and transitional — breathable fabrics essential. Kids categories are critically relevant — this is a family film event.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Super Mario Galaxy Movie screening is the most cosmically colorful family fashion moment of April — five character directions all translate beautifully into catalog pieces. Mario direction — the most universally recognizable. Red cap, red tee, blue denim co-ord — the Mario color combination is as catalog-direct as it gets. A red graphic tee with blue straight-fit jeans and a red cap is the most perfect Mario screening fit. Luigi direction — the quieter but equally valid sibling aesthetic. Green tee, blue denim, green cap — identical logic, different color. Princess Peach direction — women and girls in pink and gold — a pink co-ord, a pastel pink dress, or any pink piece with gold accessories channels Peach's regal energy beautifully. Rosalina direction — the most sophisticated character aesthetic, unique to the Galaxy film. Powder blue and silver with star accessories — a pale blue dress or blue and silver co-ord with star pendant jewellery is the most fashion-forward Mario screening look and uniquely references this specific film's setting. Bowser Jr. direction — orange-yellow and spiky chaos energy for the fans who root for the villain kid. The cosmic galaxy setting opens a broader purple and midnight navy direction — deep purple co-ords with silver star accessories for the most editorial screening approach. Kids onesies and character-print tees are the most heartwarming catalog category. Star jewellery and accessories are uniquely relevant for the Galaxy setting. April warmth means breathable cotton and lightweight fabrics in all these bold colors.

app.headline: The Galaxy Awaits. So Does This Outfit.
lines: Mario red, Luigi green, Peach pink, Rosalina blue — pick your character.
The most colorful screening of April needs the most colorful wardrobe.
Wahoo — your outfit just became the best one in the galaxy.

social_media.headline: Let's-A Go. The Fit Is Ready.
lines: Super Mario Galaxy dress code — red cap and denim for Mario, powder blue and stars for Rosalina, pink and gold for Peach. The galaxy has excellent fashion sense.
Wahoo! Red tee, blue jeans, and a star pendant for the most cosmically fun screening of the year. Super Mario Galaxy Movie, April 1st. ⭐🍄🌌

title: Moana
genres: Adventure, Comedy, Family, Fantasy, Musical, Live Action
description: The ocean calls and Moana answers — Disney's live-action reimagining of the beloved story follows Motunui's chosen daughter as she voyages beyond the barrier reef with the demigod Maui, carrying the fate of her people on a journey that is as much about discovering herself as saving her world.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Moana live-action is one of Disney's most anticipated reimaginings of 2026 — genZScore 7-9. The screening crowd spans families, Disney fans of all ages, and GenZ who grew up with the original 2016 animation. The live-action context makes the fashion even more culturally rich — real Polynesian textiles, real ocean colors, and a genuine cultural celebration of Pacific Islander fashion traditions. The poster colors — powder blue and deep ocean teal — perfectly capture the film's visual world and give direct catalog direction. Character fashion references are beautifully specific: (1) Moana — the most direct catalog reference. Coral red and warm tropical colors, a tapa-inspired printed skirt, lei accessories, natural materials. Her live-action costume will reference real Polynesian dress — woven textures, natural dyes, ocean colors; (2) Maui — bold, large, tattooed warrior energy, warm earth tones and brown; (3) The ocean itself — the dominant visual character of the film, powder blue, deep teal, aqua, and sea foam. The ocean palette from the poster is the strongest catalog signal. July in Bengaluru is peak monsoon — indoor screening but the tropical ocean palette feels perfectly appropriate for the July humidity. The tropical and Polynesian cultural references open a unique fashion direction — resort wear, ocean tones, natural fabrics, floral prints, and woven textures are all deeply relevant.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Moana live-action screening is the most ocean-inspired and tropically joyful fashion moment of July — the sea is the palette and Polynesia is the texture reference. Lead with the ocean color palette from the poster — powder blue, deep teal, and aqua are the most direct catalog signals. The live-action context makes botanical and tapa-inspired prints uniquely relevant — bold floral and tribal-adjacent prints in ocean tones reference Polynesian textile traditions beautifully without being culturally appropriative. Women in a flowing teal or ocean blue floral midi dress — the single most catalog-relevant Moana screening piece. A coral red and teal color-blocked co-ord references Moana's own costume palette directly. A powder blue linen set captures the ocean's gentle energy. Men in a bold floral or botanical printed shirt in ocean tones with linen shorts — the tropical resort aesthetic is perfect for this film. Shell motif and ocean-inspired jewellery are uniquely relevant — shell pendants, coral-toned bead necklaces, wave-motif earrings, anklets for the barefoot ocean energy. The lei reference opens floral jewellery and floral accessory directions. Flower earrings and floral hair accessories are the most Moana-coded beauty choices. Tote bags in canvas or woven textures reference the natural material world of Polynesia. Kids onesies and floral dresses in ocean tones are the most heartwarming catalog category. The July monsoon makes the ocean palette feel genuinely seasonal — dress like the rain is the ocean calling.

app.headline: The Ocean Is Calling. Dress For It.
lines: Powder blue, deep teal, coral — the ocean palette is the whole brief.
Floral prints, shell jewels, and ocean tones for Disney's most beautiful film.
Moana knew how to dress for a voyage. Now so do you.

social_media.headline: How Far I'll Go — In This Teal Dress.
lines: Moana live-action dress code — ocean blue florals, shell jewellery, and the kind of outfit that makes the sea want to help you find your way.
The ocean chose Moana. We chose this powder blue linen co-ord. Disney's Moana live-action, July 10th. 🌊🌺

title: Dhurandhar The Revenge
description: Dhurandhar returns — a high-octane Bollywood action thriller built on vengeance, power, and the kind of raw cinematic energy that makes every frame feel like a reckoning. The revenge has a name and it arrives March 18th.

fashion_keywords.reasoning: Dhurandhar The Revenge is a Bollywood action thriller — genZScore 7-8. The title and genre signal a dark, intense, power-driven aesthetic — the kind of film where the protagonist is both feared and respected. The Bollywood action thriller crowd in India dresses with maximum intention for premieres — the fan base is passionate, style-conscious, and deeply invested in the hero's aesthetic. Character fashion references for a revenge thriller: (1) The protagonist Dhurandhar — the vengeful hero, dark and powerful aesthetic, structured blacks and deep navy, intensity in every silhouette; (2) The antagonist world — dark, opulent, and menacing — deep maroon, charcoal, and gold excess; (3) The revenge thriller tone — dark, brooding, and cinematic. March in Bengaluru is warming up toward summer — the transitional weather makes light layering still possible but heavy fabrics are being set aside. The Bollywood connection means the screening crowd dresses up significantly — smart casual to elevated, with a dark and powerful color palette. Dark navy, charcoal grey, deep maroon, black, and occasional gold accents are the most catalog-relevant colors. The action thriller genre opens tactical and structured fashion directions alongside the elevated Bollywood party aesthetic.
fashion_keywords.style_notes: Dhurandhar The Revenge screening is a dark, powerful Bollywood fashion moment — dress like the protagonist just walked into the room and everyone went quiet. The hero aesthetic is the dominant catalog direction — structured, dark, and intentional. Men in a sharp black or midnight navy suit with a crisp white or deep red shirt — the Bollywood action hero screening uniform at its most powerful. A dark blazer with slim black trousers and a turtleneck in charcoal or deep maroon is equally cinematic. Pocket squares in gold or red for the elevated men's detail. Women in a deep burgundy or midnight blue satin co-ord, a structured black dress with gold accessories, or a dark dramatic silhouette that matches the film's intensity. The antagonist direction — deep maroon with gold excess — is the most editorial screening choice for women. Chain jewellery in gold or silver is the most revenge-thriller-coded accessory — layered chains, bold rings, and statement watches. The March Bengaluru transitional weather makes a structured blazer or dark jacket the perfect layer. Dark sunglasses for the premiere swagger. The overall energy is — dressed like you walked in knowing exactly what Dhurandhar came to do.

app.headline: The Revenge Has A Dress Code.
lines: Dark, sharp, and powerful — Dhurandhar's world demands your most intense look.
Black suit or midnight co-ord — dress like the reckoning is tonight.
Gold chains, dark blazer, and the confidence of someone with a plan.

social_media.headline: Dhurandhar Dressed For Revenge. So Should You.
lines: The most powerful man in the room never announces himself — but his black suit and gold chain do it for him. Dhurandhar The Revenge, March 18th.
Revenge is a dish best served in a midnight navy blazer and zero apologies. 🖤⚡
